meta.description: Grounding file mapping each claim to real evidence. Every claim below is backed by actual experiment outputs or code artifacts. Claims without evidence have been removed. Analytical estimates are explicitly marked.
meta.policy: No claim without evidence. Analytical estimates labeled as such.

claims.claim: NLP scoring functions decompose into WFA over semirings
claims.evidence_location: spectacles-core/src/scoring/*.rs — TripleMetric trait implementations (exact_match, token_count, bleu, rouge_l, regex_match, token_f1, edit_distance). Each provides score_reference(), score_automaton(), score_circuit(). score_and_verify() cross-checks all three agree.
claims.how_verified: TripleMetric trait enforces three-representation pattern at compile time. Differential testing (see claim differential-testing) confirms agreement across all representations.

claims.claim: Two-tier compilation: algebraic for F_p-embeddable semirings, gadget-assisted for tropical
claims.evidence_location: spectacles-core/src/circuit/compiler.rs — compile_algebraic() (Tier 1), compile_gadget_assisted() (Tier 2), compile_hybrid() for automatic selection.
claims.how_verified: Tier 1 uses direct homomorphism for counting/boolean semirings. Tier 2 adds comparison/range-check gadgets for tropical min-plus. Both paths exercised by differential testing and STARK proof generation.

10 random seeds × 5 metrics × 1,015 string pairs = 50,750 triple checks, plus property-based axiom checks and edge-case pairs totaling 57,518. All three representations (reference, automaton, circuit) agree on every input. 0 disagreements.
claims.notes: This supersedes the earlier '100K random pairs' claim. The actual measured count is 57,518 across structured test configurations, not a round number.

claims.claim: Real benchmark evaluation: 2,825 triple checks on MMLU/SQuAD/translation/random data, 0 disagreements
claims.how_verified: End-to-end pipeline exercised on four benchmark categories: MMLU-style (exact match), SQuAD-style (token F1), translation (BLEU-like), and random string pairs. Each input scored through reference, automaton, and circuit paths. All agree.
claims.notes: Uses representative benchmark-style data, not full datasets. Confirms pipeline correctness on realistic inputs.

claims.claim: 9 real STARK proofs generated and verified
claims.how_verified: Real end-to-end proof generation and verification for 9 circuits: fibonacci, counter, squaring, exact_match, token_count, and others. Proofs generated, serialized, and independently verified.
claims.notes: These are real measured timings, not estimates. Previous claims about proof sizes (45–85 KiB for exact match, 400–750 KiB for ROUGE-L) were analytical estimates from estimate_proof_size(); the 44 KiB mean here is from actual proof serialization on the tested circuits. Larger circuits (ROUGE-L scale) have not yet been benchmarked end-to-end.

claims.claim: PSI contamination detection operates on n-gram overlap only, NOT paraphrase memorization
claims.how_verified: PSI protocol computes |A ∩ B| over n-gram sets. This detects verbatim or near-verbatim overlap. It does NOT detect paraphrase memorization, indirect contamination, or semantically equivalent reformulations. TPR ≈ 0 for paraphrased content.
claims.what_it_does: Computes set intersection cardinality over n-gram sets via semi-honest PSI with commitment binding.
claims.what_it_does_not: Does not detect paraphrase memorization, indirect contamination, or contamination-aware fine-tuning. Threshold τ requires domain-specific calibration.
claims.notes: Certificate attests 'n-gram overlap below threshold', not 'no contamination'. Framing is 'score-verified' not 'contamination-certified'.

claims.claim: Semiring axiom verification: 125 unit tests pass plus property-based tests
claims.how_verified: 125 unit tests covering 8 core axioms (commutativity, associativity, distributivity, identities, zero annihilation) across all semiring types. Property-based tests via proptest with 256 random inputs per property. Independent Python cross-validation with 22 test functions and 500 random trials per axiom.

Paper uses 'verified specification with comprehensively tested implementation' framing. Arithmetic correctness framed as 'necessary foundation, not sufficient for evaluation trust'. Certificates attest score computation correctness, not absence of contamination.
claims.notes: Addresses overclaim critique. CompCert/CakeML comparison explicitly notes the verification gap.

claims.claim: Lean formalization covers semiring axioms only, no verified extraction to Rust
claims.how_verified: Lean 4 proofs cover KleeneSemiring axioms and partial circuit soundness. No verified extraction from Lean to Rust exists. Gap bridged empirically via property-based testing and differential testing, not machine-checked end-to-end.
claims.what_is_proved_in_lean: Semiring axioms (commutativity, associativity, distributivity, identities, annihilation). Partial circuit soundness for Tier 1.
claims.what_is_not_proved_in_lean: Full pipeline correctness. Hopcroft minimization. WFA-to-AIR compilation. Tier 2 gadget soundness. No Lean-to-Rust extraction.

sorry_count.notes: Routine sorrys targetable by omega/decide tactics. Novel sorrys require new proof techniques (Hopcroft, WFA-to-AIR).

claims.claim: Circuit trace width is 2|Q|+O(1) columns per WFA state
claims.how_verified: Analytical: num_states state columns + num_states matmul_aux columns + input/selectors/output/counter = 2·|Q| + |Σ| + 3. Cross-checked against compiler output: Exact Match (33 states → 68 columns ≈ 2×33+2), Token F1 (256 states → 514 columns ≈ 2×256+2).
claims.notes: This is an analytical formula confirmed by implementation, not a benchmark measurement. The O(1) term absorbs alphabet-size selectors and bookkeeping columns.

claims.claim: EvalSpec DSL with parser, typechecker, and formal specification
claims.how_verified: EVALSPEC_FORMAL.md contains full BNF grammar (§1), typing rules as inference rules (§2), denotational semantics mapping to formal power series (§3), WFA-expressible fragment characterization (§4). Parser (~107KB) and typechecker (~1300 lines) implement the spec.
claims.notes: Expressibility boundary is honestly documented. Non-WFA metrics explicitly marked.

claims.claim: Two math bugs found and fixed: Montgomery constant and Lagrange interpolation
claims.how_verified: Bug 1: Montgomery inverse constant corrected from 0xFFFFFFFF to 0xFFFFFFFEFFFFFFFF (Goldilocks field p = 2^64 - 2^32 + 1). Bug 2: Lagrange interpolation denominator fix. Both verified by property-based test suite and independent Python cross-validation.
claims.notes: Bugs were discovered during verification process, demonstrating value of the methodology.

removed_claims.original_claim: Proof sizes range from 45–85 KiB (exact match) to 400–750 KiB (ROUGE-L)
removed_claims.reason: These were analytical estimates from estimate_proof_size(), not measured. Real measurements show ~44 KiB mean for tested circuits. ROUGE-L-scale circuits not yet benchmarked.
Measured on synthetic test cases only. No real-world corpus benchmark to substantiate the specific range.
Line counts are not claims requiring evidence grounding. Removed as non-substantive.
Analytical scaling claim without end-to-end benchmark. Retained only as a note, not a grounded claim.

removed_claims.original_claim: Contamination ROC characteristics analyzed with false-negative characterization
removed_claims.reason: Qualitative analysis only (no measured ROC curve). Honest limitation captured in psi-contamination-scope claim instead.

removed_claims.original_claim: Boolean embedding valid for deterministic WFA execution
removed_claims.reason: Theoretical argument in paper appendix. Correctness subsumed by differential testing (claim differential-testing) which empirically confirms Boolean embedding works on all tested inputs.
